message:
Add documentation for match explanation and search explainability

- explain-match.md: New doc explaining pattern match results and confidence
- search-explainability.md: New doc on search result ranking and relevance
- cli-reference.md: Add new command references
- operator-playbook.md: Extend playbook with troubleshooting scenarios
- Sync beads issue tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-upg.11.4","title":"Improve FTS errors (invalid syntax, scopes) with actionable remediation","description":"# Task: Improve FTS errors

## Goal
Make FTS errors actionable and consistent.

## Requirements
- Invalid syntax errors:
  - stable error code
  - show where parsing failed (when safe)
  - suggest simpler query or escaping
- Scope issues:
  - clearly explain when query was limited by pane/time/retention
- Integrate with explain engine when possible.

## Testing
- Unit tests for error codes and messages.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Search failures feel self-diagnosing.

Added FtsErrorKind enum (UnbalancedQuotes, InvalidOperator, EmptyQuery, Syntax) with Display impl. New StorageError::FtsQuerySyntax variant carries structured kind + original query. Pre-validation in validate_fts_query catches empty queries and unbalanced quotes before hitting SQLite. SQLite errors categorized by message content. Added 5 unit tests for validation (empty, whitespace, unbalanced quotes, balanced quotes, valid queries). Updated existing tests.  {"id":"wa-upg.11.5","title":"Tests: search explain reasons and evidence (fixtures)","description":"# Task: Tests for search explain

## Goal
Lock in explain behavior.

## Requirements
- Fixture-based tests for each reason:
  - excluded pane
  - index lag
  - retention pruned
  - invalid syntax
  - recent GAP
- Assert:
  - reason IDs
  - evidence fields present
  - suggested commands present

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests make explain output changes intentional.

 {"id":"wa-upg.11.6","title":"E2E: search explain scenarios (excluded pane, index lag, invalid syntax)","description":"# Task: E2E search explain scenarios

## Goal
Validate explainability in a real-ish runtime.

## Scenarios
1) Excluded pane:
- configure exclude rule
- produce output
- search explain should indicate exclusion

2) Index lag:
- simulate slow index updates/backpressure
- explain indicates lag and last indexed seq/time

3) Invalid syntax:
- run an invalid query
- error code + remediation + explain hints

## Requirements
- No fixed sleeps; use wait-for/quiescence.
- Artifacts include outputs and logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E proves explain output is useful and deterministic.

 {"id":"wa-upg.12","title":"[EPIC] Backpressure-aware watcher (bounded queues + graceful degradation)","description":"# [EPIC] Backpressure-aware watcher

## Mission
Ensure the watcher remains stable under load by:
- enforcing bounded queues everywhere
- adapting polling/work under backpressure
- degrading safely (explicit GAP) rather than deadlocking or OOMing

## Why This Matters
Backpressure problems are the #1 way background daemons become unreliable:
- memory grows
- latency spikes
- the system "hangs" and users lose trust

## Scope
- Define backpressure signals and thresholds.
- Implement adaptive behavior:
  - slow down polling when queues are deep
  - batch work
  - emit health warnings
  - record GAP events when continuity can't be guaranteed
- Ensure health output makes backpressure obvious.

## Success Criteria
- Under sustained load, watcher stays responsive and memory-bounded.
- Backpressure behavior is visible and explainable.

## Testing
- Integration tests with slow consumers.
- E2E stress scenario that triggers backpressure and verifies graceful behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Watcher has explicit backpressure handling and no unbounded buffers.
- Tests and E2E validate behavior and produce good artifacts.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:51:48.333972506Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T20:51:48.333972506Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-daemon","area-ingest","area-reliability","area-tests","phase-1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.12","depends_on_id":"wa-upg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-upg.12.1","title":"Design: backpressure signals, thresholds, and degradation policy","description":"# Task: Design backpressure handling

## Goal
Specify backpressure signals and the system's response policy.

## Requirements
- Signals:
  - ingest queue depth
  - storage writer queue depth
  - detection pipeline lag
  - indexing lag
- Thresholds:
  - warning level
  - critical level
- Responses:
  - adaptive polling slowdown
  - batching
  - health warnings
  - explicit GAP insertion when continuity uncertain

## Acceptance Criteria
- Policy is deterministic and implementable.
","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:52:00.083318245Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-04T03:15:47.719177609Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-daemon","area-design","phase-1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.12.1","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-upg.12.1","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-upg.12.1","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-upg.12.1","depends_on_id":"wa-upg.12","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-upg.12.2","title":"System-wide backpressure propagation + instrumentation (no unbounded queues)","description":"# Task: System-wide backpressure propagation + instrumentation

## Goal
Ensure backpressure is handled **end-to-end** across the watcher, not just in one component.

Base backpressure behaviors are already part of the ingest/storage foundations (e.g., tailers + event bus + storage writer queues).
This task hardens the *system-level* behavior:
- propagate backpressure signals across subsystems
- prevent deadlocks/starvation
- make backpressure visible to operators and test harnesses

## Requirements
- Define a small set of standard backpressure signals (examples):
  - ingest queue depth / lag
  - storage writer queue depth / lag
  - pattern engine backlog / compute time budget
  - notification queue depth (if any)
- Propagate signals to:
  - polling scheduler (slow down responsibly)
  - batching decisions (increase batch size, reduce wakeups)
  - health/status surfaces (warnings + actionable guidance)
- Starvation avoidance:
  - ensure one noisy pane cannot starve others
  - ensure slow consumer paths do not deadlock the system

## Testing
- Integration tests:
  - synthetic slow consumers
  - multiple panes with one "noisy" producer
  - verify bounded memory + fairness

## Acceptance Criteria
- Under backpressure, the system stays responsive and memory-bounded.
- Backpressure transitions are visible in logs and health output.
- No unbounded buffers remain in the watcher hot path.

 {"id":"wa-upg.14.5","title":"Tests: explain-match trace stability, boundedness, and redaction","description":"# Task: Tests for explain-match traces

## Goal
Prove the explain-match trace is **stable**, **bounded**, and **cannot leak secrets**.

This task intentionally focuses on the *trace primitive* (wa-upg.14.2) and its invariants, not the command wiring.

## Test categories
### 1) Golden stability tests
- Golden JSON fixtures for representative rules:
  - positive match (single rule)
  - multiple matches ordering
  - no match (and optional bounded "near-miss" reason)
- Enforce deterministic ordering of:
  - matches
  - trace phases
  - extracted field keys

### 2) Boundedness tests
- Enforce maximum sizes:
  - max excerpt bytes per evidence item
  - max total trace bytes
  - explicit truncation markers (`truncated: true`, counts, etc.)
- Ensure truncation is **visible** in the trace (not silent).

### 3) Redaction / non-leak tests
- Construct inputs that include known secret-like patterns (API keys, tokens, device codes, etc.).
- Assert the trace never contains the raw secret substring.
- Assert the trace records only safe metadata (counts, hashed summaries, or `[REDACTED]`).

### 4) Schema validation tests
- Validate trace objects against the schema used by robot/human surfaces.
- Ensure schema evolution is intentional (golden fixtures catch accidental drift).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests fail loudly on:
  - ordering drift
  - schema drift
  - truncation not being explicit
  - any secret substring appearing in serialized trace output
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T22:10:46.557440590Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:53:43.611341525Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T01:53:43.611199742Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-rules","area-tests","phase-4"]}
 {"id":"wa-upg.14.6","title":"E2E: explain-match trace surfaces (rules list/test + artifacts)","description":"# Task: E2E coverage for explain-match traces

## Goal
Ensure the explain-match trace is correctly surfaced end-to-end with **rich artifacts**.

We should not duplicate the existing E2E work; instead:
- extend/validate the existing rules E2E case (`wa-4vx.10.24`) to cover the trace invariants needed by wa-upg.14

## Requirements
- In the E2E run artifacts, capture:
  - `wa robot rules test` output (JSON)
  - `wa rules test` output (TTY/plain)
  - any pack linter report output
  - logs with correlation ids
- Assertions:
  - schema-valid JSON for robot output
  - trace contains stable fields (`rule_id`, `pack_id`, evidence spans)
  - trace output is bounded and indicates truncation when applicable
  - trace output is redaction-safe (no known secret fixtures leak)

## Acceptance Criteria
- The existing rules E2E case is sufficient to validate explain-match behavior.
- When it fails, artifacts make it obvious if the break is:
  - trace core regression
  - CLI rendering regression
  - schema drift
  - pack fixture drift

Updated registry + checklist; validate_e2e_registry.sh OK.","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:25:29Z"}]}

 {"id":"wa-upg.15","title":"[EPIC] Secure distributed mode (auth + encryption + safe defaults + rigorous tests)","description":"# [EPIC] Secure distributed mode

## Mission
Make distributed mode **secure-by-default** and trustworthy:
- strong authentication
- encrypted transport
- safe bind defaults
- replay protection and DoS-resistance
- rigorous tests + E2E artifacts

This is a hardening epic: distributed mode should feel as safe and predictable as local mode.

## Background (what "distributed mode" is)
From the system design (PLAN.md §20):
- `wa-agent` runs near each remote WezTerm mux server
- it streams small `PaneDelta` segments + `Detection` events + `Gap` events to...
- a central `wa` aggregator (often on the workstation)

This reduces latency and bandwidth, and makes multi-machine fleets feasible.

## Why This Matters
A network listener is a major trust boundary.
If distributed mode is not secure-by-default, it can:
- broaden the attack surface unintentionally
- leak sensitive data via logs/streams
- allow unauthorized control/observation

We want the default posture to be:
- *off by default*
- when enabled: *safe defaults* and *explicit opt-in to danger*

## Relationship to existing distributed-mode work
Core distributed mode is tracked in `wa-nu4.4.3` (wire protocol + agent + aggregator + minimal security + E2E).
This epic upgrades that foundation to a security posture we can be confident in.

## Success Criteria
- Default bind remains localhost; remote binds require explicit opt-in + warnings.
- Remote connections are encrypted by default (TLS; optional mTLS for strong identity).
- Auth is robust and deterministic:
  - wrong/missing credentials rejected with stable error codes
  - no secret leakage in logs/artifacts
- Replay/injection resistance is enforced and test-covered.
- Rotation workflows exist and are operator-friendly (`wa doctor` can report effective security config).

## Security goals
- Confidentiality: no plaintext over the wire (when remote)
- Authentication: only authorized agents can connect
- Integrity: prevent tampering/replay
- Least astonishment: defaults are safe; dangerous binds require explicit flags
- Observability: failures are diagnosable with stable errors and artifacts

## Testing requirements
- Unit/integration:
  - protocol roundtrip + version negotiation
  - auth failures produce stable error codes
  - replay protection behavior
- Fuzzing:
  - protocol parsing should not panic
- E2E:
  - secure agent→aggregator stream scenario
  - failure injection (bad token/cert, replay, disconnect)
  - artifacts always include logs/config summaries

## Acceptance Criteria
- Distributed mode can be enabled without making the workstation a soft target.
- Auth + encryption are not optional footguns; they are the default for remote binds.
- Tests and E2E provide high confidence and excellent debugging artifacts.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T22:15:07.451814345Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T22:23:10.417423487Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-distributed","area-security","area-tests","area-ux","phase-5"]}
 {"id":"wa-upg.15.1","title":"Design: distributed threat model + auth/encryption + key management","description":"# Task: Design secure distributed mode

## Goal
Define a robust security model for distributed mode that is:
- secure-by-default
- explicit about tradeoffs and threat model
- implementable with deterministic tests

## Inputs / constraints
- Distributed mode introduces a network boundary: aggregator listens; agents connect.
- Default posture must remain local-only unless explicitly enabled.
- We must support deterministic offline E2E tests (fake agent/aggregator).

## Threat model (write down explicitly)
Consider at minimum:
- accidental exposure (operator binds to 0.0.0.0 without realizing)
- unauthorized client attempts to connect
- replay/injection of fake PaneDelta/Detection/GAP
- traffic sniffing on LAN/WAN
- credential leakage via logs/artifacts

## Decisions to make
### 1) Transport security
- Decide the baseline for remote connections:
  - TLS required when binding to non-loopback
  - optional mTLS for stronger identity
- Define certificate story:
  - self-signed/dev vs operator-provided
  - where certs live and how they are loaded

### 2) Authentication / identity
- Decide supported auth modes:
  - shared token (baseline)
  - mTLS client cert identity (recommended for serious deployments)
  - allowlist of agent identities (CN/SAN or derived agent id)
- Define constant-time comparisons and logging rules.

### 3) Replay protection and session semantics
- Define what constitutes a "session":
  - per-connection session id
  - monotonic seq per session
  - expiration/rotation behavior
- Define whether we require time-based freshness (careful: determinism/tests).

### 4) Configuration + safe defaults
- Bind defaults: localhost unless explicitly configured.
- Dangerous binds require explicit flags and produce prominent warnings.
- Config knobs must be explicit and easy to audit (`wa doctor`).

### 5) Error codes + UX
- Stable, parseable error codes for:
  - auth failures
  - TLS failures
  - replay violations
  - rate limits

## Acceptance Criteria
- A written spec exists that implementers can follow without consulting PLAN.md.
- The spec includes concrete defaults, config keys, and a crisp list of invariants.

 {"id":"wa-upg.15.2","title":"Implement encrypted transport (TLS/mTLS) for agent↔aggregator","description":"# Task: Implement encrypted transport for distributed mode

## Goal
Add encrypted transport for agent↔aggregator connections so remote distributed deployments are not plaintext.

## Requirements
- Implement TLS for aggregator listener and agent client.
- mTLS support (if selected in wa-upg.15.1):
  - verify client cert
  - optional allowlist of acceptable identities
- Safe defaults:
  - if binding to non-loopback, require TLS (or require an explicit dangerous flag to disable)
  - no "silent downgrade" to plaintext
- Logging:
  - do not log secrets, tokens, or private key material
  - log only safe metadata: peer addr, session id, negotiated protocol version

## Testing
- Unit/integration tests:
  - handshake succeeds with valid certs
  - handshake fails with invalid/expired certs
  - plaintext connection attempt is rejected when TLS required

## Acceptance Criteria
- Remote connections can be encrypted with clear, deterministic configuration.
- Failure modes are explicit and produce stable error codes.

@@ -0,0 +1,81 @@
+# Explain-Match Traces (Debugging Rule Detections)
+
+Explain-match traces help you understand why a rule matched (or did not match)
+without leaking secrets. Use them to debug false positives, false negatives, and
+rule drift.
+
+## When to use explain-match
+
+- A rule fires when it should not (false positive).
+- A rule does not fire when it should (false negative).
+- You are validating a new rule or rule pack.
+- You need to prove a detection is safe to act on.
+
+## How to run
+
+Human CLI (quick sanity checks):
+
+```bash
+wa rules list
+wa rules test "Usage limit"  # shows matches, no trace
+```
+
+Robot CLI (machine-readable + trace):
+
+```bash
+wa robot rules list
+wa robot rules test "Usage limit" --trace
+```
+
+Notes:
+- `wa robot rules test --trace` currently returns a minimal trace
+  (`anchors_checked`, `regex_matched`). Full trace output is planned.
+- `--pack` is accepted for robot rules test, but filtering is not yet implemented.
+
+## Mental model (phases)
+
+Think of explain-match as a sequence:
+
+1. **Eligibility gates**: agent type, dedupe, and other rule-level gates.
+2. **Anchor checks**: fast string matches to narrow candidates.
+3. **Regex match + captures**: full regex evaluation and named captures.
+4. **Evidence assembly**: excerpts, spans, and extracted fields (redacted).
+
+## Trace fields (full format)
+
+When full explain-match traces are enabled, each match emits a `MatchTrace` with
+these fields:
+
+- `pack_id`, `rule_id`, `extractor_id`, `matched_text`, `confidence`
+- `eligible`: whether the rule passed eligibility gates
+- `gates[]`: `{ gate, passed, reason }` for each gate
+- `evidence[]`: items with `kind`, `label`, `span`, `excerpt`, `truncated`
+- `bounds`: `{ max_evidence_items, max_excerpt_bytes, max_capture_bytes,
+  evidence_total, evidence_truncated, truncated_fields }`
+
+Interpretation tips:
+- `span` uses byte offsets in the original input string.
+- `evidence.kind` typically includes anchors, matches, and captures.
+- `truncated=true` on evidence means the excerpt was clipped to bounds.
+- `bounds.evidence_truncated=true` indicates evidence was dropped for size.
+
+Default bounds (from `TraceOptions::default()`):
+- `max_evidence_items`: 8
+- `max_excerpt_bytes`: 160
+- `max_capture_bytes`: 120
+
+## Redaction and privacy
+
+All excerpts and captured values are passed through the policy redactor. Secrets
+are replaced with `[REDACTED]` (or `[REDACTED:pattern]` when debug markers are
+enabled). Do not expect raw secrets to appear in traces.
+
+## Incident bundles
+
+`wa diag bundle` includes rule traces for recent events when available:
+
+- `traces/rule_traces.json`
+
+These traces include redacted `matched_text` and extracted fields to help debug
+rule behavior safely and share artifacts without leaking secrets.
+

+## Flow 4: search explain → fix
+
+Use this for missing or incomplete search results.
+
+1) Run safe checks:
+
+```bash
+wa search "error"
+wa search fts verify
+wa doctor
+```
+
+2) If the index is inconsistent, rebuild:
+
+```bash
+wa search fts rebuild
+```
+
+3) For detailed reason codes and remediation, see `docs/search-explainability.md`.
+

+

 ```bash

@@ -0,0 +1,164 @@
+# Search Explainability and Indexing Lag Troubleshooting
+
+This guide explains how to interpret search explain output and fix common FTS
+search issues. It is designed for operators who need to diagnose missing or
+incomplete search results without risky actions.
+
+## Quick triage (safe)
+
+1. Confirm the watcher is running and panes are visible.
+
+```bash
+wa status
+wa list
+```
+
+2. Run a normal search and verify the pane scope.
+
+```bash
+wa search "error"
+wa search "error" --pane 3
+```
+
+3. Check FTS index health.
+
+```bash
+wa search fts verify
+```
+
+4. If results are still missing, collect diagnostics.
+
+```bash
+wa doctor
+wa db check -f plain
+```
+
+5. If the index is inconsistent, rebuild it (safe but may be slow).
+
+```bash
+wa search fts rebuild
+```
+
+## Search explain output shape
+
+Search explain results are ranked reasons with evidence and suggestions. Each
+reason has a stable code, confidence score, and remediation hints.
+
+Fields to expect:
+- `query`: the original FTS query
+- `pane_filter`: pane ID if a `--pane` filter was used
+- `total_panes`, `observed_panes`, `ignored_panes`
+- `total_segments`: total indexed segments
+- `reasons[]`: ordered by confidence, each with `code`, `summary`, `evidence[]`,
+  and `suggestions[]`
+
+Plain text output is rendered like:
+
+```text
+Search explain for query: "error"
+  Pane filter: 3
+  Panes: 6 total (5 observed, 1 ignored)
+  Indexed segments: 241
+
+2 potential issue(s):
+
+  1. [FTS_INDEX_INCONSISTENT] FTS index is inconsistent for 1 pane(s). Some content may not be searchable.
+     pane_3_segments: 120
+     pane_3_fts_rows: 95
+     Suggestions:
+       - Run diagnostics: wa doctor
+       - The FTS index may need rebuilding.
+```
+
+## Reason codes and what to do
+
+Use the reason codes below to self-diagnose and resolve issues:
+
+- `NO_INDEXED_DATA`: No output has been captured yet.
+  Start `wa watch` and ensure panes are active and not excluded.
+- `PANE_EXCLUDED`: The requested pane is excluded by config rules.
+  Adjust `ingest.panes` include/exclude rules and re-check `wa list`.
+- `PANES_EXCLUDED`: One or more panes are excluded from capture.
+  Review pane filters and confirm the desired panes are observed.
+- `PANE_NOT_FOUND`: The pane ID is not known to the watcher.
+  Verify with `wa list`; the pane may be closed or undiscovered.
+- `FTS_INDEX_INCONSISTENT`: FTS rows do not match segment counts.
+  Run `wa search fts verify`, then `wa search fts rebuild` if needed.
+- `CAPTURE_GAPS`: Gaps were detected in captured output.
+  Check `wa events --rule-id gap` and consider lowering poll interval.
+- `RETENTION_CLEANUP`: Retention has removed older content.
+  Review retention settings and increase retention if needed.
+- `STALE_PANES`: Observed panes have not been seen recently.
+  Confirm the watcher is running and panes are still open.
+- `NARROW_TIME_RANGE`: Data spans less than one minute.
+  Wait for more data to accumulate before re-running search.
+
+## Indexing lag troubleshooting
+
+Indexing lag usually shows up as low FTS row counts or inconsistent panes.
+Use the sequence below to diagnose and correct it.
+
+1. Confirm data is being captured.
+
+```bash
+wa list
+wa search fts verify
+```
+
+2. If segment counts are increasing but FTS rows lag, run diagnostics.
+
+```bash
+wa doctor
+```
+
+3. If the index is inconsistent, rebuild it.
+
+```bash
+wa search fts rebuild
+```
+
+4. If lag persists, check for capture gaps or backpressure warnings.
+
+```bash
+wa events --rule-id gap
+wa status
+```
+
+## Adjusting pane include/exclude rules
+
+Pane filters live under `ingest.panes`. Excludes always win. If any include
+rules exist, panes must match at least one include rule to be observed.
+
+Example:
+
+```toml
+[ingest.panes]
+
+[[ingest.panes.include]]
+id = "ssh_panes"
+domain = "SSH:*"
+
+[[ingest.panes.exclude]]
+id = "secret_cwd"
+cwd = "/home/user/private"
+```
+
+After editing config:
+
+```bash
+wa config validate
+wa config show --effective
+wa list
+```
+
+## Safe diagnostics checklist
+
+Use these steps before any destructive actions:
+
+1. `wa status` to confirm watcher health.
+2. `wa list` to confirm panes and ignore reasons.
+3. `wa search fts verify` to detect index inconsistencies.
+4. `wa doctor` for a full health snapshot.
+5. `wa db check -f plain` for DB health.
+6. `wa db repair --dry-run` to preview fixes (if needed).
+
